purpose: POC1-v4 layer-aware trace-based state-only vs dependency-aware proxy comparison
comment: proxy compare on 48 categorized prompts x 3 layers for critical bucket v2

The logistics manager rerouted shipments after a regional delay forced several trucks to wait near the depot

Why did the router send these tokens to different experts when the prompt looked simple?

Can a sparse expert layer reveal communication pressure before workers begin computing?
Which signal is more useful: current queue size or routing dependency structure?
When should a system prioritize a smaller bucket over a larger hot bucket?
Note: GPU-0 handled 18 routes, GPU-1 handled 7, and the barrier moved unexpectedly
The report says latency dropped 12%, but only after reordering the expert dispatch phase
Experiment A uses state-only priority; experiment B uses dependency-aware priority
Router output: experts=[3, 14, 27], weights=[0.41, 0.22, 0.09]
A short message, a JSON key, and a code token appeared in the same prompt
Please summarize: queue pressure, co-active experts, and the likely slow destination

aggregate.grouping_focus_recommendation: max_to_avg_bucket_ratio currently gives the largest top-1 divergence contrast; the lower side shows more state/dependency separation.
aggregate.structure_conclusion: largest grouped divergence contrast is under max_to_avg_bucket_ratio
aggregate.short_conclusion: state-only and dependency-aware proxies sometimes diverge; dependency structure is worth testing further

layer_0.grouping_focus_recommendation: max_to_avg_bucket_ratio currently gives the largest top-1 divergence contrast; the lower side shows more state/dependency separation.
layer_0.skew_conclusion: divergence does not increase in higher-skew samples in this batch
layer_0.structure_conclusion: largest grouped divergence contrast is under max_to_avg_bucket_ratio
layer_0.short_conclusion: state-only and dependency-aware proxies sometimes diverge; dependency structure is worth testing further

layer_15.grouping_focus_recommendation: active_destination_count currently gives the largest top-1 divergence contrast; the higher side shows more state/dependency separation.
layer_15.structure_conclusion: largest grouped divergence contrast is under active_destination_count
layer_15.short_conclusion: state-only and dependency-aware proxies sometimes diverge; dependency structure is worth testing further

layer_8.grouping_focus_recommendation: max_to_avg_bucket_ratio currently gives the largest top-1 divergence contrast; the lower side shows more state/dependency separation.
layer_8.structure_conclusion: largest grouped divergence contrast is under max_to_avg_bucket_ratio
layer_8.short_conclusion: state-only and dependency-aware proxies sometimes diverge; dependency structure is worth testing further

layer_focus.layer_focus_recommendation: Prioritize layer_15; it shows the strongest dependency-aware ranking divergence. Deprioritize layer_8 for near-term proxy experiments.
